mission.title: Install and Configure Ovn Kubernetes on Kubernetes
mission.description: OVN-Kubernetes is a robust networking solution for Kubernetes clusters that leverages Open Virtual Networking (OVN) and Open vSwitch (OVS). Installing OVN-Kubernetes enables advanced networking capabilities for your Kubernetes applications.

steps.title: Create OVN-Kubernetes Namespace
steps.description:
Create a dedicated namespace for OVN-Kubernetes using the following command:
```bash
kubectl create namespace ovn-kubernetes
```

steps.title: Deploy OVN-Kubernetes Components
steps.description:
Apply the necessary manifests to deploy OVN-Kubernetes components. Use the following commands:
```bash
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ovn-kubernetes/ovn-kubernetes/v1.12.0/manifests/ovn-k8s-cni-overlay.yaml
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ovn-kubernetes/ovn-kubernetes/v1.12.0/manifests/ovn-k8s-controller.yaml
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ovn-kubernetes/ovn-kubernetes/v1.12.0/manifests/ovn-k8s-setup.yaml
```

steps.title: Verify Installation
steps.description:
Check that the OVN-Kubernetes pods are running correctly:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n ovn-kubernetes
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of OVN-Kubernetes will show all pods in the 'ovn-kubernetes' namespace as running. You can also verify the resource limits have been applied by checking the pod specifications.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ovn-kubernetes/ovn-kubernetes/v1.12.0/manifests/ovn-k8s-cni-overlay.yaml
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ovn-kubernetes/ovn-kubernetes/v1.12.0/manifests/ovn-k8s-controller.yaml
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ovn-kubernetes/ovn-kubernetes/v1.12.0/manifests/ovn-k8s-setup.yaml

uninstall.title: Remove OVN-Kubernetes Components
uninstall.description:
To uninstall OVN-Kubernetes, delete the components using the following commands:
```bash
kubectl delete -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ovn-kubernetes/ovn-kubernetes/v1.12.0/manifests/ovn-k8s-cni-overlay.yaml
kubectl delete -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ovn-kubernetes/ovn-kubernetes/v1.12.0/manifests/ovn-k8s-controller.yaml
kubectl delete -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ovn-kubernetes/ovn-kubernetes/v1.12.0/manifests/ovn-k8s-setup.yaml
kubectl delete namespace ovn-kubernetes
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade OVN-Kubernetes
upgrade.description:
To upgrade OVN-Kubernetes, apply the new version of the manifests. For example, to upgrade to version 1.13.0:
```bash
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ovn-kubernetes/ovn-kubernetes/v1.13.0/manifests/ovn-k8s-cni-overlay.yaml
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ovn-kubernetes/ovn-kubernetes/v1.13.0/manifests/ovn-k8s-controller.yaml
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ovn-kubernetes/ovn-kubernetes/v1.13.0/manifests/ovn-k8s-setup.yaml
```

troubleshooting.title: Pod Not Starting
troubleshooting.description:
If any pods are not starting, check the pod logs for errors:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> -n ovn-kubernetes
```

troubleshooting.title: Network Issues
troubleshooting.description:
If you experience network issues, ensure that the OVN components are running and check their status:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n ovn-kubernetes
```

troubleshooting.title: Configuration Errors
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter configuration errors, verify the configuration files and ensure they are correctly applied. You can also check the events for the namespace:
```bash
kubectl get events -n ovn-kubernetes
```

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a running Kubernetes cluster and kubectl installed and configured to interact with your cluster.
